what: WJPr: WJP data and visualizations tools — WJPr-package
title: WJPr: WJP data and visualizations tools — WJPr-package
text: WJPr package contains important data visualization tools used Data Analytics Unit World Justice Project. Version 0.1.0 comes wide range visualization functions replicating image charts Country Reports. Additionally, can find Rule Law Index scores data factors subfactors complementary data.

title: WJPr: WJP data and visualizations tools — WJPr-package
text: Maintainer: Carlos . Toruno Paniagua ctoruno@worldjusticeproject.org Authors: . Santiago Pardo Gonzalez spardo@worldjusticeproject.org

what: Plot a Bar Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_bars
title: Plot a Bar Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_bars
text: wjp_bars() takes data frame specific data structure (usually long shaped) returns ggplot object bar chart following WJP style guidelines.

title: Plot a Bar Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_bars
code: wjp_bars(   data,   target,   grouping,   labels = NULL,   colors,   cvec = NULL,   direction = "vertical",   stacked = F,   lab_pos = NULL,   expand = F,   order = NULL,   width = 0.9,   ptheme = WJP_theme() )

title: Plot a Bar Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_bars
text: data Data frame containing data plot target String. Column name variable supply values plot. grouping String. Column name variable supplies grouping values. labels String. Column name variable containing value labels display plot. colors String. Column name variable contains color grouping. cvec Named vector colors apply bars. direction String. bars plotted "horizontal" "vertical" way? stacked Boolean. TRUE, bars stacked top per group. Default FALSE. lab_pos String. Column name variable contains coordinates value labels. expand Boolean. TRUE, plot give extra space value labels. Default FALSE. width Numeric value 0 1. Width bars percentage space bar. ptheme ggplot theme function apply plot. default, function applies WJP_theme() order_var String. Column name variable contains custom order labels.

title: Plot a Bar Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_bars
Plot a Bar Chart following WJP style guidelines — wjp_bars
code: # Always load the WJP fonts if not passing a custom theme to function wjp_fonts(path2SP) #> Error in eval(expr, envir, enclos): object 'path2SP' not found  # Preparing data data2plot <- gpp %>% select(country, q1a, q1b, q1c) %>%    mutate(across(!country, ~case_when(.x == 1 | .x == 2 ~ 0, .x == 3 | .x == 4 ~ 1))) %>%    group_by(country) %>%   summarise(across(everything(), \(x) mean(x, na.rm = TRUE))) %>%    mutate(across(!country, \(x) x*100)) %>%   pivot_longer(!country, names_to = "group", values_to = "value2plot") #> Error in gpp %>% select(country, q1a, q1b, q1c) %>% mutate(across(!country,     ~case_when(.x == 1 | .x == 2 ~ 0, .x == 3 | .x == 4 ~ 1))) %>%     group_by(country) %>% summarise(across(everything(), function(x) mean(x,     na.rm = TRUE))) %>% mutate(across(!country, function(x) x *     100)) %>% pivot_longer(!country, names_to = "group", values_to = "value2plot"): could not find function "%>%"    # Plotting chart  wjp_bars(data = data2plot, target = "value2plot", grouping = "country", colors = "group") #> Error in data %>% dplyr::mutate(labels_var = "") %>% dplyr::rename(target_var = all_of(target),     grouping_var = all_of(grouping), colors_var = all_of(colors),     lab_pos = all_of(lab_pos), order_var = all_of(order)): could not find function "%>%"
